commands.review_summary:
The PR adds MiniMax-M3 service_tier=priority injection when the existing fastMode flag is true, plus focused MiniMax stream-wrapper tests.

PR surface: Source +10, Tests +59. Total +69 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: current source passes auto fast mode as a function and sibling wrappers resolve it dynamically, while the PR only checks strict true. I did not run a live MiniMax account in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed because the remaining blockers are PR-branch fixes plus real endpoint proof, not a safe cleanup close or automated repair lane.

target.title: feat(minimax): opt M3 into MiniMax priority lane via fastMode
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

MiniMax-M3 exposes a paid low-latency `service_tier: "priority"` lane on its
Anthropic-compatible endpoint, but OpenClaw has no way to opt a model's
requests into it. Operators running latency-sensitive M3 workloads cannot
trade spend for the faster lane today.

## Why This Change Was Made

Gate the priority lane behind the existing per-model `extraParams.fastMode`
flag. When `fastMode` is set on an M3 model, `createMinimaxThinkingDisabledWrapper`
injects `service_tier: "priority"` into the request payload. Scope and non-goals:

- Opt-in only. Default behavior is unchanged when `fastMode` is unset/false.
- M3 only. M2.x models gain no priority benefit, so the flag is a no-op there.
- A `service_tier` already set by an earlier wrapper or caller is preserved.

## User Impact

Operators can opt MiniMax-M3 requests into MiniMax's priority lane by setting
`fastMode` in the model's params, reducing latency at higher cost. No impact
on any model or caller that does not set the flag.

## Evidence

New focused tests in `src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/minimax.test.ts`
cover: priority injected for M3 when enabled, not injected when disabled,
no-op for M2.x even when enabled, and preservation of a pre-set `service_tier`.

```
$ pnpm vitest run src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/minimax.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  18 passed (18)
```

`tsc -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit` reports no new errors in the changed files.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: jzakirov
